title: Mike Mullen on Office Hours
summary: U.S. Navy Admiral (ret.) Mike Mullen, Chairman of the joint Chiefs of Staff from 2007 to 2011, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about global leaders who are moving the needle, worrying trends in U.S. government, and golf and fly fishing.
body: U.S. Navy Admiral (ret.) Mike Mullen, Chairman of the joint Chiefs of Staff from 2007 to 2011, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about global leaders who are moving the needle, worrying trends in U.S. government, and golf and fly fishing. The latest from Belfer See more Gallery 1 of 4 2 of 4 3 of 4 4 of 4 Admiral (ret.) Michael "Mike" Mullen finished his 43 years of service in the U.S. Navy serving as the 17th Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, the highest ranking officer in the United States Armed Forces. Mullen previously served as the Navy's 28th Chief of Naval Operations, U.S. Naval Forced Europe, Commander, Allied Joint Force Command Naples, and as the 32nd Vice Chief of Naval Operations. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: In this interview, retired U.S. Navy Admiral Mike Mullen, former Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff (2007-2011), discusses significant global leaders who are influencing international affairs and expresses concern over troubling trends within the U.S. government. The conversation also touches on personal interests such as golf and fly fishing. The article provides background on Mullen's distinguished 43-year naval career, including his roles as Chief of Naval Operations and commander of U.S. Naval Forces Europe. The context includes references to related Belfer Center content on international security, such as analyses of the Sino-American military contest in Asia and the U.S.-China military balance in space, highlighting the ongoing focus on strategic competition and defense policy.
result.main_content_zh: 本文是对美国海军退役上将、前参谋长联席会议主席迈克·马伦（2007-2011年任职）的访谈。马伦在访谈中探讨了那些正在影响国际局势的全球领导人，并对美国政府内部令人担忧的趋势表示了关切。谈话还涉及高尔夫和飞蝇钓鱼等个人兴趣。文章介绍了马伦43年的杰出海军生涯，包括他曾担任海军作战部长、美国海军欧洲司令部司令等职务。内容背景关联了贝尔弗中心其他关于国际安全的材料，例如对中美在亚洲的军事竞争及太空军事平衡的分析，体现了对战略竞争和国防政策的持续关注。
keywords_en: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
